flashcards.back: The Block Started by Symbol segment. It holds uninitialized and zero-initialized global and static variables. The OS initializes it to zero at runtime.

flashcards.front: Which two segments grow towards each other?
flashcards.back: The Heap (grows upwards) and the Stack (grows downwards).

flashcards.front: What is the key difference in management between stack and heap memory?
flashcards.back: Stack memory is automatically allocated/freed on function call/return. Heap memory must be manually allocated (malloc) and freed (free) by the programmer.

flashcards.front: What does the Text segment contain and what are its permissions?
flashcards.back: It contains the executable code of the program. It is typically read-only and shared between processes.

flashcards.front: Where are the command-line arguments (argv) and environment variables stored?
flashcards.back: At the high end of the address space, often just above the stack.
